seed: certainty vs curiosity; order_vs_chaos; rising_signal; bottom_to_top; tide against channels; receding_edges; straight_vs_curved; cage; relief_dread | abstract variation request 18; target batch count 200
title: Tides of Contrast
reasoning: The emotional tone revolves around the tension between certainty and curiosity, reflecting the struggle and interplay between order and chaos. The palette of signal green, electric blue, dead black, static gray, and hot magenta highlights this contrast: the darker tones represent certainty and structure, while the vibrant hues symbolize curiosity and exploration. The central collision occurs at row 4, where the bright colors intersect with darker fields, creating an abrupt tension that echoes the themes of rising signals and receding edges. The layout directs the viewer’s eye from the bottom to the top, reminiscent of a tide’s movement against static patterns, while incorporating broken pixels to introduce imperfection and reflect real-world disruptions in order. The composition maintains negative space, allowing the viewer to breathe amidst the chaos, symbolizing relief from dread while sparking intrigue.

layout.name: empty_room
layout.description: Mostly empty space with one meaningful object off-center.

composition.name: light_vs_weight
composition.idea: A small bright shape fights against a heavy dark mass.

referenceExample.title: Tiny Hope Under Pressure
referenceExample.pattern: dark frame with single bright center pixel

generationRules: Pick 1 pattern type for emotion A.
Pick 1 pattern type for emotion B.
Pick 1 shape seed as the central object.
Apply 1 to 6 broken pixels to create imperfection.
Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the mood is panic, rage, urgency, euphoria, or pressure.
Use the shape seed as an abstract organizing metaphor, not as a literal icon.
Make the two selected pattern types visibly collide, overlap, interrupt, or trade space.
Prefer 4 to 8 distinct hex colors, with at least one quiet field color and one accent color.
Keep the 8x8 grid readable at thumbnail size by preserving clear large-scale structure.
Let one emotion dominate 55 to 70 percent of the square unless the selected layout demands balance.
Use broken pixels as intentional disruptions, scars, repairs, leaks, or false echoes.
Avoid perfectly even distribution unless the chosen composition is control, serenity, clarity, or certainty.
Give the main collision a location: center, edge, corner, diagonal, border, or channel.
generationSpec.negativeSpaceRule: Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the chosen emotional tone needs dense pressure.
